content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA

CALIFORNIA MEDICAL ASSISTANCE COMMISSION
1121 L STREET, SUITE 300
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 324-2726

January 17, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir or Madam:

This is to request the opinion of your Commission concerning any conflict of interest with my duties as a member of the California Medical Assistance Commission (CMAC).

I was recently appointed as a Commissioner with CMAC (Welfare and Institutions Code Section 14165, et seq). At the same time, I am a lobbyist registered in accordance with the Government Code. I wish to make certain that my duties as a Commissioner do not create a conflict of interest prohibited by statute or regulation.

CMAC duties involve health care matters connected with the State's Medi-Cal program. Primarily, the Commission negotiates contracts with hospitals to provide inpatient services to Medi-Cal beneficiaries.

As a lobbyist I represent no health care related entities. My clients include:

Association of Reporter Training Schools
California Barber College Association
Aaron Reed and Associates
Reading Specialists of California
Sport Fishing Association of California

I am concerned that my duties as a CMAC Commissioner do not create a prohibited conflict of interest with my lobbying activities or my lobbyist friends and have the following questions:

1) May I accept revenue from other lobbying firms for on-going consulting and other work unrelated to health care issues and medical providers?

2) Are there prohibitions preventing activities with friends of mine who are also registered lobbyists but who are not involved in health care activities or CMAC duties? For example, can I accept lunches or gifts from these friends?

3) Essentially, even though I will not have involvement with any lobbyist friends who represent hospitals or other health care entities having business with CMAC, are there any restrictions upon my relationships with these friends simply because I am now a Commissioner with CMAC?

I appreciate your advice and look forward to receiving your reply.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
Frederick L. Shanbour
Commissioner

FLS:BC:jcl
